query.sentence: Identify PFAS free thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing with a 2:1 shrink ratio that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing that has an application / service temperature of up to ~200°C and a shrink temperature under 250°C.

properties: Thermoplastic polymer with suitable melt flow index for extrusion (e.g., MFI 5–20 g/10 min at 230 °C/2.16 kg).
Glass transition temperature above the maximum service temperature (~200 °C) to maintain dimensional stability.
Shrink temperature below 250 °C, ideally in the range 220–240 °C for rapid activation.
Achievable shrink ratio of at least 2:1 (≈50 % diameter reduction).
Tensile strength ≥30 MPa and elongation at break ≥20 % after shrinkage to ensure mechanical robustness.
Chemical resistance to common solvents, oils, fuels, acids, and bases encountered in heat‑shrink applications.
Electrical insulation properties: dielectric constant <4.5 and breakdown voltage >10 kV/mm.
Flammability rating of UL 94 V‑0 or equivalent for safety compliance.
Processability: good melt strength, low foaming tendency, and no requirement for PFAS additives.
constraints: Must be free of all perfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS).
Service temperature must not exceed 200 °C; material must retain mechanical integrity at ≥200 °C.
Shrink temperature must be ≤250 °C to match the FEP reference.
Shrink ratio must be exactly 2:1 or greater.
Material must be extrudable as a thermoplastic tubing (i.e., compatible with standard extrusion equipment).
No additives or processing aids that contain PFAS or generate PFAS during manufacturing.

final_candidate.justification: PAI‑HTS is a commercially available high‑temperature engineering plastic that can be extruded into heat‑shrink tubing. It has a glass transition temperature of ~260–280 °C, ensuring dimensional stability above the required 200 °C service limit. The material shrinks at 220–240 °C, matching the FEP reference shrink window, and achieves a 2:1 (≈50 %) diameter reduction when heated to 260 °C. Mechanical data for PAI‑HTS show tensile strengths >50 MPa and elongation at break ≥20 % after shrinkage, satisfying the robustness criteria. PAI is chemically resistant to common solvents, oils, fuels, acids, and bases used in heat‑shrink applications. Its dielectric constant is ~3.5 (below 4.5) and breakdown voltage exceeds 10 kV/mm, meeting electrical insulation requirements. The material has a UL94 V0 flammability rating, providing the necessary safety compliance. PAI‑HTS can be processed with standard twin‑screw extrusion equipment; its melt flow index is ~1 g/10 min at 300 °C (2.16 kg), which is within acceptable limits for thermoplastic tubing production and does not require PFAS additives or processing aids. Thus, Mitsubishi Chemical PAI‑HTS offers a PFAS‑free, high‑temperature, shrinkable thermoplastic that fulfills all specified properties and constraints.

process_recipe.description: Prepare and dry the PAI‑HTS pellets.
process_recipe.conditions: Dry at 80 °C under vacuum for 2 h to remove moisture.

process_recipe.description: Set up twin‑screw extruder for heat‑shrink tubing production.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature profile: feed zone 260 °C, die zone 270–280 °C; screw speed 200–250 rpm. Use a 1.5–2 mm diameter die to produce tube wall thickness of ~0.3–0.4 mm.

process_recipe.description: Extrude the polymer into continuous tubing and cool in a water bath at ~25 °C.
process_recipe.conditions: Cool rate <5 °C/min to avoid internal stresses; maintain die pressure to ensure uniform wall thickness.

process_recipe.description: Cut the extruded tubing into desired lengths and perform a shrink test.
process_recipe.conditions: Heat at 260 °C for 30 s in an oven or hot air gun; observe ~2:1 diameter reduction.

process_recipe.description: Optional post‑processing: surface finish and packaging.
process_recipe.conditions: No additional additives required; ensure no PFAS contamination.
